content.problemStatement:
You are positioned at the start of an array where each element represents the maximum number of steps you can jump forward from that position. Your goal is to determine if you can reach the last position of the array.

At each index iii, you can jump any number of steps between 1 and A[i]A[i]A[i] forward. For example, if A[i]=3A[i] = 3A[i]=3, you can jump 1, 2, or 3 positions ahead from index iii.


Input Format

First line contains NNN - the length of the array
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​


Output Format

Print "YES" if you can reach the last index, "NO" otherwise

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Array = [2, 3, 1, 1, 4]
Possible path: Index 0 -> Index 1 -> Index 4
At index 0, jump 1 step to index 1
At index 1, jump 3 steps to reach index 4 (end)

Explanation:
Array = [3, 2, 1, 0, 4]
No matter what path you take, you'll always reach index 3
At index 3, maximum jump length is 0, making it impossible to reach index 4
